title: Omar Khayy占퐉
content:
(TO THE OMAR KHAYY占폦 CLUB)

혻혻혻혻Great Omar, here to-night we drain a bowl
혻혻혻혻Unto thy long-since transmigrated soul,
혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻Ours all unworthy in thy place to sit,
혻혻혻혻Ours still to read in life's enchanted scroll.

혻혻혻혻For us like thee a little hour to stay,
혻혻혻혻For us like thee a little hour of play,
혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻A little hour for wine and love and song,
혻혻혻혻And we too turn the glass and take our way.

혻혻혻혻So many years your tomb the roses strew,
혻혻혻혻Yet not one penny wiser we than you,
혻혻혻혻The doubts that wearied you are with us still,
혻혻혻혻And, Heaven be thanked! your wine is with us too.

혻혻혻혻For, have the years a better message brought
혻혻혻혻To match the simple wisdom that you taught:
혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻Love, wine and verse, and just a little bread - 
혻혻혻혻For these to live and count the rest as nought?

혻혻혻혻Therefore, Great Omar, here our homage deep
혻혻혻혻We drain to thee, though all too fast asleep
혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻In Death's intoxication art thou sunk
혻혻혻혻To know the solemn revels that we keep.

혻혻혻혻Oh, had we, best-loved Poet, but the power
혻혻혻혻From our own lives to pluck one golden hour,
혻혻혻혻And give it unto thee in thy great need,
혻혻혻혻How would we welcome thee to this bright bower!

혻혻혻혻O life that is so warm, 'twas Omar's too;
혻혻혻혻O wine that is so red, he drank of you:
혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻Yet life and wine must all be put away,
혻혻혻혻And we go sleep with Omar - yea, 'tis true.

혻혻혻혻And when in some great city yet to be
혻혻혻혻The sacred wine is spilt for you and me,
혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻To those great fames that we have yet to build,
혻혻혻혻We'll know as little of it all as he.
meaning:
"Omar Khayy占퐉" is a quintessential example of Richard Le Gallienne's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
